title: Marvell CEO: Demand for Products Continuing to Accelerate with Data Center Revenue at $1.5B
text: Marvell Technology posted record revenue of $2.075 billion, representing 37% year-over-year growth. The company's data center segment drove performance, generating $1.518 billion in revenue, 73% of total sales and growing 38% annually. The semiconductor firm projects particularly ambitious targets for future growth. Marvell anticipates approaching $11 billion in sales for 2027, with 2028 revenue potentially reaching $15 billion compared to Wall Street's $13 billion forecast. The company also projects earnings per share exceeding $5. CEO Matt Murphy stated: Looking ahead, we see demand for our products continuing to accelerate, and as a result, our data center revenue growth forecast for next year is now higher than prior expectations. Marvell announced acquiring Celestial AI to strengthen its optical interconnect capabilities, positioning itself to address the next infrastructure bottleneck in AI data centers. The surge reflects growing demand for custom AI accelerators designed specifically for inference workloads, complementing NVIDIA's traditional dominance in AI training chips.
